stories.title: Google is working on a new AI chip designed to make Gemini more efficient
stories.summary: Alphabet, Google's parent company, is reportedly working on a new chip designed to make its Gemini models run much more efficiently.
stories.extract:
Alphabet, Google’s parent company, is designing a new server chip to help its in-house Gemini models operate more efficiently.
The new chip, internally dubbed “Frozen v2,” is slated to be released sometime in 2028, The Information reported, citing anonymous sources. According to the report, the chip could be between six and 10 times more efficient than Google’s existing AI chips, measured by the number of tokens generated per unit of power.
In a response to TechCrunch, the company didn’t directl

stories.title: China’s AI models have Trump’s AI world at war with itself
stories.summary: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Over the weekend, several current and former advisors to Presi
stories.extract:
China’s AI models have Trump’s AI world at war with itself
Kimi and other free models from China have again been seen as a wake-up call. But for what?
This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here.
Over the weekend, several current and former advisors to President Donald Trump on AI publicly lobbed insults at the country’s leading AI companies. David Sacks, the president’s AI and crypto “czar” until March,

stories.title: Anthropic’s landmark $1.5B copyright settlement is approved
stories.summary: The final approval settles one case, but it doesn't resolve the broader issue of using copyrighted works to train AI models.
stories.extract:
Anthropic can finally start cutting checks to a group of authors and book publishers that sued the AI lab over copyright infringement. A federal judge gave final approval Monday of Anthropic’s landmark $1.5 billion settlement of a class action copyright lawsuit, Reuters reported.
Judge William Alsup of the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California issued a preliminary approval of the settlement last year, after ruling that Anthropic had illegally downloaded and stored millions

stories.title: Trump’s latest AI czar has already resigned
stories.summary: The director role for the Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) has become a revolving door since David Sacks left his position as czar.
stories.extract:
Chris Fall, the director of the Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI), has resigned, the agency confirmed to multiple news outlets.
He was appointed just three months ago after the last appointee, Collin Burns, left in less than a week, The Washington Post reported at the time. Burns was reportedly “pushed out” of the job in April because he previously worked for Anthropic and the Trump administration had been battling with the company, sources told the Post.
No reason was given for Fal

stories.title: AI is more likely than humans to form biases when hiring
stories.summary: The next time you apply for a job, AI may screen your résumé before any human sees it. But there’s good reason to question whether AI will judge you fairly. Researchers already know that LLMs pick up
stories.extract:
AI is more likely than humans to form biases when hiring
AI doesn’t just learn stereotypes from its training. It can cook up new ones, too.
The next time you apply for a job, AI may screen your résumé before any human sees it. But there’s good reason to question whether AI will judge you fairly. Researchers already know that LLMs pick up human biases from their training data. New research suggests that LLMs can also develop their own biases from experience—and stereotype job applicants more than

stories.title: Jeff Bezos and UK government invest in £2bn British startup CuspAI
stories.summary: Company aims to develop AI software that cuts research times and use of rare metals in chipmakers’ supply chains Business live – latest updates Amazon’s founder, Jeff Bezos, and the UK government have
stories.extract:
Amazon’s founder, Jeff Bezos, and the UK government have invested in a £2bn British artificial intelligence startup that is aiming to become the “search engine for rare materials” that accelerates the next wave of technological breakthroughs.
The Cambridge-based CuspAI has raised $450m (£330m) in funding from investors including Bezos and the government’s sovereign AI fund, valuing the two-year-old business at $2.6bn.
The company has launched the AI Materials Foundry, a coalition of more than 48

stories.title: SEM-Guided Low-kV FIB Finishing for Leading-Edge Semiconductor Failure Analysis
stories.summary: Discover how the ZEISS Crossbeam 750 FIBSEM sets a new benchmark for precise TEM lamella prep, tomography, and advanced nanofabrication. This delivers better resolution, better SNR, larger usable FOV,

stories.title: No longer token economy? SenseTime bets on ‘task economy’ as token prices set to drop
stories.summary: The commercialisation of artificial intelligence is poised to transition from a “token economy” to a “task economy” as token prices decline in the coming years, according to Chinese AI company SenseTi
stories.extract:
No longer token economy? SenseTime bets on ‘task economy’ as token prices set to drop
AI’s true commercial value will shift towards a ‘task economy’ where users pay for completed, complex tasks, says Xu Li.
The commercialisation of artificial intelligence is poised to transition from a “token economy” to a “task economy” as token prices decline in the coming years, according to Chinese AI company SenseTime.
“The pricing of tokens will inevitably drop, much like the cost of telecoms data did two

stories.title: Chinese robot makers’ lament: if we only had a better ‘brain’, and more data
stories.summary: Chinese robotics companies lack both sufficient data and a good “brain” to improve the interaction of their products with the physical world, according to industry insiders at the World Artificial Int
stories.extract:
Chinese robot makers’ lament: if we only had a better ‘brain’, and more data
Experts at WAIC discuss the bottlenecks preventing embodied AI from improving robots’ real-world interactions
Chinese robotics companies lack both sufficient data and a good “brain” to improve the interaction of their products with the physical world, according to industry insiders at the World Artificial Intelligence Conference (WAIC), which concluded on Monday in Shanghai.
The most critical challenge for the embodied

stories.title: IEEE Program Helps Girls In India See a Future In STEM
stories.summary: Roughly half the world’s population is female, but the STEM fields don’t reflect that. The 2024 U.N. Global Education Monitoring Report on gender found that about 35 percent of STEM college graduates
stories.extract:
Roughly half the world’s population is female, but the STEM fields don’t reflect that. The 2024 U.N. Global Education Monitoring Report on gender found that about 35 percent of STEM college graduates were women. The proportion hasn’t increased much in more than a decade.
When it comes to STEM careers, the percentage is even lower. Women made up about 28 percent of the global STEM workforce in 2024, according to the World Economic Forum.
There are myriad factors for the discrepancy, including a l

stories.title: The Space Force is now seeking to buy up to $30 billion in rocket launches
stories.summary: The Trump administration is asking the Space Force to do a lot. This will require more launches.
stories.extract:
It seems as though $5.6 billion wasn’t enough. That was the news from the US Space Force on Friday, when military officials announced they were tripling the maximum value of one of the service’s National Security Space Launch contracts to $17 billion.
The expansion of the Space Force’s National Security Space Launch (NSSL) Phase 3 contract comes as the Pentagon signals rising demand for military satellite launches. The NSSL program is set up to allow Space Systems Command, which oversees the Spa

stories.title: Why China’s open-weight AI model Kimi K3 is sparking anxiety in Silicon Valley
stories.summary: Chinese start-up Moonshot AI’s Kimi K3 has narrowed the country’s artificial intelligence gap with the US to “a matter of weeks”, fuelling jitters in Silicon Valley over its lead in frontier AI as Chi
stories.extract:
Why China’s open-weight AI model Kimi K3 is sparking anxiety in Silicon Valley
Analysts say Beijing-based start-up Moonshot’s 2.8 trillion-parameter large language model could narrow the gap with the US within ‘weeks’
The Beijing-based unicorn released the Kimi K3, a massive 2.8 trillion-parameter large language model, late last week. According to the company, the model featured native vision capabilities and was built for long-horizon coding, knowledge work and complex reasoning tasks.
The mode

stories.title: Apple Crumble: a tasty Knives Out-inspired mystery that lets you snoop on your own family
stories.summary: The developer of Duck Detective reveals details of her ‘sad grandma game’ follow-up: a whodunnit – or who-plans-to-do-it? – with a darker edge Someone wants to kill grandma. In this Knives Out - inspi
stories.extract:
Someone wants to kill grandma. In this Knives Out-inspired mystery game, each of your family members is suspicious in their own way: your stressed mum, your “midlife crisis uncle” and especially that stranger in your bedroom. Yikes!
While Apple Crumble is still a mystery, it is mechanically different from Happy Broccoli Games’ previous game, Duck Detective, in which the player solves crimes in a small office. Here you are largely snooping through your family home. “I love the idea of having a wa

stories.title: AI-altered images on birdwatching forums putting research at risk
stories.summary: Experts warn increase in enhanced photos on birding platforms creating fake sightings, threatening credibility of tool used by scientists For many birdwatchers, recording a species outside its normal
stories.extract:
For many birdwatchers, recording a species outside its normal range is the holy grail. In the UK, the discoveries often make national headlines. The western reef heron, for example, usually found in Africa and southern Europe, was spotted in a seaside town in north Wales in June, and widely celebrated on birding forums.
But a new scourge is threatening to disrupt the fun: AI slop.
Scientists are appealing to birders to limit their use of AI when editing images over fears that it could undermine

stories.title: The Organisms That Make Earth’s Harshest Places Home
stories.summary: Extremophiles that thrive in the most unforgiving environments aren’t just biological curiosities. Understanding their resilience has many implications for us. The post The Organisms That Make Earth’s
stories.extract:
The Organisms That Make Earth’s Harshest Places Home
Life has scarcely found a boundary on Earth that it can’t push. While much of life’s diversity exists in lush, bountiful habitats like tropical rainforests and coral reefs, even the most brutal corners of the planet are also occupied. The organisms that thrive in extreme environments — blistering temperatures, crushing pressures, corrosive acid — are what we call “extremophiles.”
Most of these imperiled pioneers are rugged microbes, such as ba

stories.title: India's first privately developed rocket reaches orbit on dramatic debut launch
stories.summary: "On the first attempt, reaching orbit, I never thought it was possible."
stories.extract:
Indian space officials celebrated the debut flight of Skyroot Aerospace’s Vikram-1 rocket, India’s first fully commercial satellite launcher, as a “grand success” Saturday after an on-target climb into a 280-mile-high orbit following liftoff from an island spaceport in the Bay of Bengal.
The Vikram-1 lifted off from India’s primary spaceport on Sriharikota Island at 1:35 am EDT (06:35 UTC) Saturday, around midday at the launch base along India’s southeast coast. The launch was delayed more than

stories.title: We’re Squandering LEDs’ Potential to Save Our Night Skies
stories.summary: In the chill of a London spring night, under overcast skies, iconic Trafalgar Square opens around me. Admiral Nelson rises on his pedestal, the National Gallery rests behind, the church of St Martin-i
stories.extract:
We’re Squandering LEDs’ Potential to Save Our Night Skies
Bad decisions have turned efficient technology into a driver of light pollution. There is a better way
In the chill of a London spring night, under overcast skies, iconic Trafalgar Square opens around me. Admiral Nelson rises on his pedestal, the National Gallery rests behind, the church of St Martin-in-the-Fields sits nearby. From the 13th century, the site served as the Royal Mews for hawks and then horses. By 1844, it was a public spac

stories.title: Q&A: What the EU’s carbon market review means for climate action
stories.summary: The European Commission has put forward new plans to cut emissions under the EU carbon... The post Q&A: What the EU’s carbon market review means for climate action appeared first on Carbon Brief .
stories.extract:
Q&A: What the EU’s carbon market review means for climate action
Orla Dwyer
07.20.26Orla Dwyer
20.07.2026 | 5:11pmThe European Commission has put forward new plans to cut emissions under the EU carbon market more slowly, from 2031 onwards.
On 17 July, the commission presented its long-awaited proposal for reform of the EU’s Emissions Trading System (ETS).
It recommended a number of changes, including giving companies free allowances to cover their emissions for longer than previously planned, co

stories.title: ‘A public health emergency waiting to occur’: how US industry groups spent years fighting who gets to measure toxic air
stories.summary: Air polluters try to ensure toxic emissions happen far from monitors – but the people who live nearby still feel it Kimbrelle Kyereh lives just a few hundred feet from the massive chemical storage tan
stories.extract:
Kimbrelle Kyereh lives just a few hundred feet from the massive chemical storage tanks in St Rose, in south-eastern Louisiana. Oily fumes sometimes drift into her home, bringing headaches, nausea and insomnia. On one such night, she grabbed her son at 3am and fled to an all-night Ihop.
In 2023, after years of coping with the noxious odors, Kyereh began collecting air samples in her yard for a local environmental justice group. But soon after, Louisiana passed an industry-backed law that sharply

stories.title: Australian ski resorts smash July climate records with temperatures 12C above average
stories.summary: Resorts in New South Wales and Victoria face a warm and dry start to the season with only sporadic falls of natural snow in many alpine areas Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get
stories.extract:
Alpine ski resorts in New South Wales and Victoria saw climate records shattered at the weekend with maximum temperatures up to 12C above average.
Ski areas of Mount Hotham and Falls Creek set July records for their hottest daytime and night-time temperatures and Thredbo also set a new hottest July day, according to the Bureau of Meteorology.
The records come after a challenging start for Australia’s ski season with dry and warm temperatures seeing only sporadic falls of natural snow in many alp

stories.title: Slowdown in Arctic ice melt ends after largest winter decline on record
stories.summary: Scientists recorded slower reduction over past 20 years, but study finds this was ‘temporary rather than sustained’ A previously recorded slowdown in Arctic sea ice decline has ended, according to a n
stories.extract:
A previously recorded slowdown in Arctic sea ice decline has ended, according to a new study, with the largest year-to-year wintertime reduction ever recorded.
In a study published last summer, researchers found that melting sea ice in the Arctic had slowed down over the past 20 years, with no statistically significant decline in its extent recorded between 2005 and 2024.
Given the increase in global temperatures during this period, the finding was surprising.
However, the researchers said natur

stories.title: States join fight against Trump administration’s wind farm blockade

The geothermal company was alre

stories.title: FDA still focused on lettuce supplier as source of parasite, despite faulty test result
stories.summary: The FDA said Monday it is still focused on lettuce from Taylor Farms as the source of a multistate outbreak of a diarrhea-causing parasite, despite a faulty test result.
stories.extract:
WASHINGTON — Federal health officials said Monday they remain focused on lettuce from Taylor Farms as the source of a multistate outbreak of a diarrhea-causing parasite, despite inaccurate test results that the government briefly publicized over the weekend.
Food and Drug Administration officials on Monday said a laboratory test incorrectly identified a positive result for cyclospora on a sample of lettuce from Taylor Farms. The FDA posted the finding to its website on Saturday but then said Sun

stories.title: STAT+: The next step of site-neutral, and a fiery PBM hearing
stories.summary: Medicare proposes to reduce payments to hospitals for certain drugs and imaging services.
stories.extract:
This is the online version of STAT’s weekly email newsletter Health Care Inc. Sign up here.
Hello, everyone! I’ve seen upwards of 50,000 public comments on proposed health care regulations, but the 200,000 comments (!) on the SEC’s proposal to eliminate quarterly financial reports is jaw-dropping. Yes, a lot of them seem like automated templates from interest groups, but still. Some poor soul has to sift through them. I’d like to see you try to send 200,000 comments here (please don’t): [email p

stories.title: STAT+: A most-wanted fugitive appears to have been secretly working as a biotech executive
stories.summary: A former doctor accused of sexual assault but on the run for 20 years appears to have been living a secret life as a biotech executive.
stories.extract:
One of Rhode Island’s most-wanted fugitives — a former doctor convicted of sexual assault but on the run for 20 years — appears to have been living a secret life as a biotech executive.
Ronald Fischer, 70, was arrested last week by federal and Rhode Island authorities after they tracked and boarded a 56-foot sailboat cruising off the coast of New Jersey. The former anesthesiologist disappeared in 2005 while on trial for first-degree sexual assault.
The boat was registered under the name Richard

stories.title: Geopolitics ‘chills’ British biotech IPO plans, despite $2B in Q2 VC funds
stories.summary: Geopolitical uncertainty has continued to “chill” IPO sentiment among British biotechs, an industry leader has told Fierce, even as the sector saw second-quarter venture capital funding reach a five-y
stories.extract:
Geopolitical uncertainty has continued to “chill” IPO sentiment among British biotechs, an industry leader has told Fierce, even as the sector saw second-quarter venture capital funding reach a five-year high.
British biotechs secured 2.05 billion pounds ($2.7 billion) between April and June 2026, according to a July 20 report from U.K. trade body the BioIndustry Association (BIA). The majority of this haul came from the 1.6 billion ($2.1 billion) series B for the Alphabet-backed Isomorphic Labs

stories.title: Kolon’s cell-based gene therapy fails phase 3 osteoarthritis trial
stories.summary: A phase 3 trial of Kolon TissueGene’s cell-based gene therapy has missed its co-primary endpoints. With a second late-stage study scheduled to report data in October, the biotech is holding off on det
stories.extract:
A phase 3 trial of Kolon TissueGene’s cell-based gene therapy has missed its co-primary endpoints. With a second late-stage study scheduled to report data in October, the biotech is holding off on determining the next steps for the osteoarthritis candidate.
The failed phase 3 trial tested TG-C, a drug candidate containing human allogeneic chondrocytes and an irradiated human embryonic kidney-derived cell line stably expressing TGF-β1. Authorities in South Korea authorized the medicine in 2017, o

stories.title: Understanding Agentic AI in Clinical Development
stories.summary: Earlier this year, my colleague Matt Harrington suggested that clinical leaders should stop asking whether their organizations have an “AI strategy”, not because strategy doesn’t matter, but because t
stories.extract:
By Rajneesh Patil, Head of AI and Innovation for Clinical Development, Syneos Health
Earlier this year, my colleague Matt Harrington suggested that clinical leaders should stop asking whether their organizations have an “AI strategy”, not because strategy doesn’t matter, but because the question often produces the wrong conversation.
Too often, discussions about AI become conversations about platforms, vendors, models and roadmaps. What leaders really need to understand is how AI is being used t

stories.title: Tourism and infrastructure threaten habitat of India’s Himalayan salamanders
stories.summary: Every monsoon season, in the misty hills of Darjeeling, in West Bengal, eastern India, the elusive Himalayan salamander emerges from beneath the forest floor. The ancient amphibians (Tylototriton hima
stories.extract:
Every monsoon season, in the misty hills of Darjeeling, in West Bengal, eastern India, the elusive Himalayan salamander emerges from beneath the forest floor.
The ancient amphibians (Tylototriton himalayanus) engage in a delicate dance, a mating ritual that lasts up to 90 minutes before they attach their eggs to semi-submerged vegetation, such as denseflower knotweed (Persicaria glabra). The survival of the next generation of salamanders is, however, increasingly at risk as their critical wetlan

stories.title: Peru’s only penguin species at risk from upcoming El Niño and bird flu, experts warn
stories.summary: SAN JUAN DE MARCONA, Peru — First, there was the H5N1 avian influenza of 2022, then El Niño arrived in 2023, disrupting coastal waters and fish availability. For Peru’s Humboldt penguins, the only spe
stories.extract:
- The Humboldt penguin is the only penguin species to breed in Peru, inhabiting the country’s coastal deserts. But since 2022, the species’ has been under pressure from the spread of avian influenza, followed by El Niño.
- Between 2022 and 2025, the population of Humboldt penguins has dropped by two-thirds across 34 islands and peninsulas on the Peruvian coast.
- Experts monitoring the species say penguins are abandoning their nests as they already feel the impacts of the upcoming El Niño episod

stories.title: Renato Moraes de Jesus was a pioneer of Atlantic Forest restoration in Brazil
stories.summary: In 1977 a young forest engineer arrived at a neglected tract of Atlantic Forest in northern Espírito Santo. A mining company had acquired the property as a source of hardwood for railway sleepers. Two
stories.extract:
- Renato Moraes de Jesus helped transform the Vale Natural Reserve in Espírito Santo into a leading center for Atlantic Forest conservation, research, and restoration.
- Over more than three decades, he established scientific collections, laboratories, nurseries, and long-term research programs while protecting nearly 23,000 hectares of forest.
- He later helped design the restoration of Fazenda Bulcão and the technical program that became Instituto Terra.
- A practical forester and scientist, h

stories.title: Phytoform partners with major seed breeders to bring AI-powered plant design to corn crops
stories.summary: The companies will target "whole-plant corn architecture" to improve harvestable yield and productivity. The post Phytoform partners with major seed breeders to bring AI-powered plant design to corn c
stories.extract:
UK-based gene editing startup Phytoform is working with two of the world’s major seed companies—Beck’s Hybrids, the largest family-owned seed retailer in the US, and international breeder RAGT—to improve corn genetics for growers.
The companies will target “whole-plant corn architecture” to improve harvestable yield and productivity. While the specific trait remains undisclosed, Phytoform cofounder and CEO William Pelton describes the goal as improving how the plant grows and performs at a struc

stories.summary: The Rourkela startup makes indigenous ROVs and AUVs to inspect bridges, pipelines and undersea cables, and won a Rs 66 crore Indian Navy contract under iDEX.

stories.extract:

- Adani Energy's ₹3,050 crore acquisition of IntelliSmart is less about manufacturing smart meters and more about owning the long-term contracts that run them.
- The rollout is becoming increasingly difficult to ignore because financially stretched DISCOMs need accurate billing, lower losses and better visi

stories.title: AI is shrinking video game development teams to one
stories.summary: AI is helping solo developers thrive in Turkey’s gaming industry, but writers, artists, and junior programmers are being left behind.
stories.extract:
As a teen, Emirhan Gül was a keen gamer, so he was thrilled to join a gaming startup in Istanbul as a developer after university. Over five months, as Turkey rode the wave of mobile gaming, he worked on the design of 23 games, and soon experienced burnout.
“Budgets were high, there was a lot of work, and they hired anyone, even if they didn’t know much,” the 24-year-old told Rest of World. But “the idea of churning out a game a week didn’t really appeal to me much. It wasn’t a culture I particul

stories.title: Kanwaljit Singh on the consumer-brands bet, the anti-power-law fund, and turning down twice the money
stories.summary: The post Kanwaljit Singh on the consumer-brands bet, the anti-power-law fund, and turning down twice the money appeared first on The Ken .
stories.extract:
- In 2017, Kanwal Singh walked away from tech investing to raise Fireside Ventures, a fund only for Indian consumer brands, when almost no one believed India had a consumer story worth venture capital
- Unable to convince global institutions, he raised his first fund from the consumer families who had actually built India's brands, from the Mariwalas and Premji Invest to Unilever and ITC.
- He rejects the venture power law: rather than chase one 100X outlier, Fireside works hands-on through a "c

stories.summary: The Reserve Bank of India said its measures allowing lenders more room to offer attractive rates on overseas deposits to non-resident Indians have garnered $17.4 billion until last Friday.

stories.title: ‘Good growth in every postcode’: Burnham’s economic to-do list in seven charts
stories.summary: From industrial strategy and defence to housing and living costs, we lay out the challenges facing the new PM Andy Burnham becomes prime minister as Britain contends with a series of global economic s
stories.extract:
Andy Burnham becomes prime minister as Britain contends with a series of global economic shocks and years of weak growth in living standards, fuelled by underinvestment and deep regional divisions.
Before his arrival in Downing Street, the Labour leader pledged to deliver “good growth in every postcode” by transferring power from Westminster to local communities. But with the public finances under pressure, and time running out before the next general election, the task is not straightforward.
H

stories.title: South Africa Secures $1.5 Billion World Bank Loan for Infrastructure Upgrades
stories.summary: South Africa secured a $1.5 billion World Bank loan to help modernize the country’s infrastructure and create hundreds of thousands of jobs in the continent’s largest economy.

stories.title: AI job worries grow. But some human skills can’t be replaced by machines | Gaynor Parkin and Dave Winsborough
stories.summary: The fear of becoming obsolete is a rising source of anxiety as artificial intelligence emerges in the workplace. Staying strongly connected with others will help The modern mind is a column where expe
stories.extract:
Earlier this year Paul* discovered via a flurry of media stories that his business group had been targeted for job cuts, alongside a number of other public-sector agencies. For him, this was the second time, after his technical management role was restructured in 2024. While that process was difficult enough, the comms this time were highlighting the integration of AI as a cost-saving tool. “They say it’s not about firing people, but hey, my whole team will go.”
For Paul, the timing could not ha

stories.title: Why a modest US interest rate rise won’t change much for most businesses
stories.summary: Rates may rise, but don’t be too concerned – a small increase is unlikely to change major decisions on investment or hiring With the appointment of a new Federal Reserve chair, the latest concern is t
stories.extract:
With the appointment of a new Federal Reserve chair, the latest concern is that interest rates will go up in the next few months. That decision will depend on many factors, including inflation, jobs and the economy’s overall growth. But when that happens, watch out! The media will go nuts and the president will go ballistic. Everyone treats every Fed meeting as an economic earthquake. But for most small businesses, it isn’t.
A 25-basis-point increase doesn’t meaningfully change borrowing costs f

stories.title: Stone pelted on India’s first hydrogen train? Indian Railways responds to viral video
stories.extract:
2 min readUpdated: Jul 21, 2026 12:56 AM IST
												 
												Stone pelting on India’s first hydrogen train: Indian Railways has issued a clarification after a video went viral on social media claiming that a stone-pelting incident had taken place on the country’s first hydrogen train. Flagged off by Prime Minister Narendra Modi on July 17, the train is operating between Jind and Sonipat in Haryana. The 89-km-long section is a part of Northern Railway’s Delhi Division.

stories.title: Russia-Ukraine war: 4 Indian sailors among 10 killed as missile hits ship
stories.extract: Shubhajit Roy, Diplomatic Editor at The Indian Express, has been a journalist for more than 25 years now. Roy joined The Indian Express in October 2003 and has been reporting on foreign affairs for more than 17 years now. Based in Delhi, he has also led the National government and political bureau at The Indian Express in Delhi — a team of reporters who cover the national government and politics for the newspaper. He has got the Ramnath Goenka Journalism award for Excellence in Journalism ‘2016.

stories.title: More than half of Odisha’s PVTG adult women anaemic, says a study by Utkal University
stories.source: India Latest News: Top National Headlines Today & Breaking News | The Hindu
stories.summary: Among PVTGs, the Hill Kharia shows the highest stunting prevalence at 82.76%, a distressing figure that signifies a critical public health emergency, the study raises alarm
stories.extract:
A sizeable section of Odisha’s particularly vulnerable tribal groups (PVTGs) suffers from anaemia, affecting 37% of adult men and over 53% of adult women, while indicators such as blood pressure and random sugar levels point to an early onset of metabolic risks, according to a Utkal University study released on Monday (July 20, 2026).
“The growth, nutritional, and health profile of Odisha’s PVTGs reflects a complex interplay of undernutrition, emerging lifestyle changes, increasing metabolic ris

stories.title: Gujarat destroys drugs worth ₹2,153 crore at Dahej
stories.source: India Latest News: Top National Headlines Today & Breaking News | The Hindu
stories.summary: “The Gujarat Police have dealt a major blow to international drug smuggling networks through decisive action against drug mafias and cartels,” Mr. Sanghavi
stories.extract:
Narcotic substances worth ₹2,153 crore, seized in 867 cases across Gujarat, were destroyed at Dahej in Bharuch district on Monday, with Deputy Chief Minister Harsh Sanghavi saying the State’s crackdown had dealt a major blow to international drug trafficking networks.
The destruction exercise, organised at Enviro Infrastructure Limited’s facility in Dahej, covered 13,249.127 kg of drugs and 5,71,028 bottles of narcotic substances seized by 24 police units, including the CID Crime and the Anti-Te

stories.title: Six killed in floods, landslips in Northeast
stories.source: India Latest News: Top National Headlines Today & Breaking News | The Hindu
stories.summary: Railway authorities cancelled, diverted, or short-terminated several passenger train services in Assam. At least 25 people have been killed across four States since the last week of June
stories.extract:
Floods and rain-induced landslips claimed six human lives across Assam and Meghalaya over the last 48 hours, taking the death toll in the northeastern region to at least 25 since the last week of June.
The flood situation in Assam worsened on Monday (July 20, 2026), affecting 3.63 lakh people across 16 districts in the eastern, central, western, and southern parts of the State. Assam State Disaster Management Authority (ASDMA) officials said five people died—three men and two girls—in Jorhat and

stories.title: Australian ski resorts smash July climate records with temperatures 12C above average
stories.summary: Resorts in New South Wales and Victoria face a warm and dry start to the season with only sporadic falls of natural snow in many alpine areas Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get
stories.extract:
Alpine ski resorts in New South Wales and Victoria saw climate records shattered at the weekend with maximum temperatures up to 12C above average.
Ski areas of Mount Hotham and Falls Creek set July records for their hottest daytime and night-time temperatures and Thredbo also set a new hottest July day, according to the Bureau of Meteorology.
The records come after a challenging start for Australia’s ski season with dry and warm temperatures seeing only sporadic falls of natural snow in many alp

stories.title: Australia news live: Qantas apologises for four-hour wait on Sydney tarmac; Barnaby Joyce unleashes on dog in 7.30 interview
stories.summary: Airline says communication ‘could have been better’ after about 250 passengers waited hours for flight to Denpasar to leave. Follow today’s news live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily ne
stories.extract:
Qantas apologises to passengers stuck on tarmac for more than four hours
Qantas has apologised to about 250 passengers who were stuck on the tarmac in Sydney for four-and-a-half hours on Monday afternoon while the airline dealt with multiple separate aircraft issues.
Flight QF43 to Denpasar had been scheduled to leave at 4.20pm yesterday.
A passenger on the flight called ABC Radio Sydney last night, after four hours of waiting on the tarmac, saying:
They’ve come back with multiple messages and t

stories.title: ‘Front groups and fellow travelers’: state department attacks US left over Cuba
stories.summary: New report denounces Havana and claims ‘campaign of subversion’ has infiltrated highest levels of US government As part of the Trump administration’s continuing attack on left-leaning groups, the US s
stories.extract:
As part of the Trump administration’s continuing attack on left-leaning groups, the US state department has listed several activists and organizations as “front groups and fellow travelers” for Cuba, including Hasan Piker and Amazon Labor Union founder Chris Smalls.
In a 100-page report released on Monday, the state department denounces Cuba as the “capital of 21st-century communism”, accusing the Cuban government of carrying out a “campaign of subversion” that has “infiltrated the highest reach

stories.title: Police block thousands of 'cockroach' protesters marching on India's parliament
stories.summary: Some protesters demanding education reforms said they were injured in clashes with police who banned the march.
stories.extract:
Police block thousands of 'cockroach' protesters marching on India's parliament
- Published
Thousands of supporters of the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP), a youth-led protest movement seeking education and employment reforms, defied a police ban and tried to march on parliament to demand India's education minister resign.
Police wielding canes used tear gas to disperse crowds in central Delhi. Several demonstrators said they were injured in the cane charges. Police did not comment. Some in the crow

stories.extract:
UK’s new PM Andy Burnham vows to end years of instability
After being formally appointed by King Charles, Burnham promised a 10-year plan to restore political stability and revive the economy
Andy Burnham took office on Monday as Britain’s new prime minister, promising a 10-year plan to restore political stability and revive the economy while acknowledging that six other UK leaders have tried and failed to do so in the past decade.

stories.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran, as Trump warns of retaliation for deaths of soldiers
stories.summary: The US says the attacks were aimed at degrading Iran's capabilities to attack ships in the Strait of Hormuz.
stories.extract:
US launches fresh strikes on Iran, as Trump warns of retaliation for deaths of soldiers
- Published
The US military says it has begun a new round of strikes against Iran to "further degrade" its capabilities to attack ships in the Strait of Hormuz.
The strikes - the 10th consecutive night of attacks - come after US President Donald Trump vowed to punish Iran for the deaths of US soldiers, two of whom have been identified by the Pentagon.
Trump earlier said Iran had been hit "very hard" the previ

The DoJ said it reviewing whether Harvard University is allowing Chinese donors to create scholarships that exclude American students

stories.extract:
Trump imposes 50% tariffs on Canadian goods, citing disputes over cars, alcohol and cheese
The US said Canada was one of the only nations other than China that hit back against Trump’s previous tariffs and must be held accountable
US President Donald Trump on Monday imposed 50 per cent tariffs on most Canadian goods, declaring that Canada has unfairly discriminated against American cars, alcohol and dairy products.

stories.title: Yemen's Houthis announce 'maritime embargo' on Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: The Houthis say the embargo begins immediately and is a response to a Saudi blockade of ports and airports in Yemen.
stories.extract:
Yemen's Houthis announce 'maritime embargo' on Saudi Arabia
- Published
Yemen's Houthis have announced a "maritime embargo" against Saudi Arabia.
The Iran-backed group's military spokesman said it would begin immediately and was in retaliation for a Saudi blockade of ports and airports in Houthi-controlled north-western Yemen.
He gave few details about the embargo, but a Houthi media official said they were closing the Bab al-Mandab Strait - the Red Sea's southern gateway - to Saudi vessels.

stories.title: Tourism and infrastructure threaten habitat of India’s Himalayan salamanders
stories.summary: Every monsoon season, in the misty hills of Darjeeling, in West Bengal, eastern India, the elusive Himalayan salamander emerges from beneath the forest floor. The ancient amphibians (Tylototriton hima
stories.extract:
Every monsoon season, in the misty hills of Darjeeling, in West Bengal, eastern India, the elusive Himalayan salamander emerges from beneath the forest floor.
The ancient amphibians (Tylototriton himalayanus) engage in a delicate dance, a mating ritual that lasts up to 90 minutes before they attach their eggs to semi-submerged vegetation, such as denseflower knotweed (Persicaria glabra). The survival of the next generation of salamanders is, however, increasingly at risk as their critical wetlan

stories.title: Peru’s only penguin species at risk from upcoming El Niño and bird flu, experts warn
stories.summary: SAN JUAN DE MARCONA, Peru — First, there was the H5N1 avian influenza of 2022, then El Niño arrived in 2023, disrupting coastal waters and fish availability. For Peru’s Humboldt penguins, the only spe
stories.extract:
- The Humboldt penguin is the only penguin species to breed in Peru, inhabiting the country’s coastal deserts. But since 2022, the species’ has been under pressure from the spread of avian influenza, followed by El Niño.
- Between 2022 and 2025, the population of Humboldt penguins has dropped by two-thirds across 34 islands and peninsulas on the Peruvian coast.
- Experts monitoring the species say penguins are abandoning their nests as they already feel the impacts of the upcoming El Niño episod

stories.title: Renato Moraes de Jesus was a pioneer of Atlantic Forest restoration in Brazil
stories.summary: In 1977 a young forest engineer arrived at a neglected tract of Atlantic Forest in northern Espírito Santo. A mining company had acquired the property as a source of hardwood for railway sleepers. Two
stories.extract:
- Renato Moraes de Jesus helped transform the Vale Natural Reserve in Espírito Santo into a leading center for Atlantic Forest conservation, research, and restoration.
- Over more than three decades, he established scientific collections, laboratories, nurseries, and long-term research programs while protecting nearly 23,000 hectares of forest.
- He later helped design the restoration of Fazenda Bulcão and the technical program that became Instituto Terra.
- A practical forester and scientist, h

stories.title: Inhaling high-dose CO2 clears Alzheimer’s proteins from the brain
stories.summary: Intermittingly inhaling a high dose of carbon dioxide seems to remove the proteins amyloid and tau, which are implicated in Alzheimer's disease, from the brain by boosting its glymphatic system
stories.extract:
Inhaling high levels of carbon dioxide intermittently for half an hour flushes proteins linked to Alzheimer’s disease out of the brain, a small study has found. Exposure to the gas changes how blood vessels in the brain constrict and dilate while we’re awake, mimicking activity that occurs during deep sleep. This then turbocharges the glymphatic system to remove the build-up of waste products that could cause harm.
A 2025 study suggests the same approach could help with Parkinson’s disease, by c

stories.title: Watch John Glacier Live Out Her 007 Fantasies in New “Bond Girl” Video
stories.summary: The clubby track is the first single off her upcoming album Lack of Joy is Death
stories.extract:
John Glacier has released the first single off her next album, Lack of Joy is Death. “Bond Girl” takes a more upbeat approach to the East London artist’s ambient rap with flows buoyed by a clubby beat as she raps about jet-setting and talking to Vogue (a likely reference to her actual British Vogue interview in May). The song also arrives with a 007-themed music video directed by Léa Ceheivi, where Glacier completes a risky mission. Check it out below.
Revisit our review of Glacier’s 2025 debut

stories.title: Detailed maps of European wetlands reveal overlooked patches of ecosystems
stories.summary: Nature, Published online: 20 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02211-2 Satellite images have been analysed by a machine-learning algorithm to produce high-resolution maps of wetland environments — a b
stories.extract:
- NEWS AND VIEWS
Detailed maps of European wetlands reveal overlooked patches of ecosystems
The world’s wetlands are precious: they cover only 5–8% of Earth’s land surface, but store 20–30% of its soil carbon. Human activities have disturbed these environments, causing substantial carbon losses. Writing in Nature, Kovács et al. report high-resolution maps of European wetlands that provide an essential foundation for restoration work (G. M. Kovacs et al. Nature 655, 932–941; 2026).

stories.title: Smuggling charges against NIH virologists trigger political uproar
stories.summary: Nature, Published online: 20 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01995-7 Other researchers say that non-infectious viral samples in luggage posed no risk, but might have violated import rules.
stories.extract:
Two virologists at the US National Institutes of Health (NIH) are facing charges related to the alleged smuggling of non-infectious monkeypox virus (MPXV) and other biological samples into the United States, in a case that has become a flashpoint for legal and political concerns around biosecurity.
The charges centre on samples that NIH scientists Vincent Munster and Claude Kwe were carrying when they arrived in Detroit, Michigan, in January after doing research in the Republic of the Congo. Sam

stories.title: The science of foresight: how to future-proof your research
stories.summary: Nature, Published online: 20 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02231-y Anticipating from the start the various ways a research programme can succeed or fail can avoid problems later. Here’s how to do
stories.extract:
Every research programme is a bet on the future. Yet, scientists rarely examine the assumptions behind those bets. Decisions about grants, infrastructure, hiring, regulation and training often presume that certain technologies will mature, specific skills will be needed, the public will accept the resulting innovations and few risks will materialize. Yet these assumptions are rarely stated, let alone tested or revised in a systematic way1.
The consequence is a mismatch between current research a

stories.title: Beastie Boys’ Mike D Adds Tour Dates in North America and Europe
stories.summary: His Mike D 5D band heads out on its first major trek in late summer
stories.extract:
Mike D is taking his Mike D 5D band on the road behind his forthcoming solo album Thank You. The Beastie Boys alum has added 10 new shows apiece in North America and Europe, expanding a five-show run announced last month. Check out the full itinerary below.
Mike D is joined in the band by Will Graefe, Eddie and Milo Ruscha, and his sons Skyler and Davis Diamond. The album arrives August 28, via Capitol, two days before the tour kick-off in Boston.
Mike D 5D Tour:
08-30 Boston, MA - Royale

stories.title: Melissa Auf der Maur Unveils First New Music in 16 Years
stories.summary: On Bass Womb Room, several new compositions thread together an unvaulted set of ’90s demos
stories.extract: Melissa Auf der Maur is back with her first (sort of) new album in 16 years. On Bass Womb Room: My ’90s 4-Track Demos x Field Recordings, the former Hole bassist links a trove of unreleased, living-room demos she made in the 1990s with eight new interludes composed for a forthcoming photography exhibition. Those interstitials are partly built around old field recordings Auf der Maur made by placing a handycam on her amp during her time with Hole and as a touring member of the Smashing Pumpkins.
